rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VALERIE E. SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/16/2022 at request of counsel. Examiner has only received a copy of the 01/19/2022 notice. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LUCILA O. PALMIANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The conservator and attorneys request compensation in the total amount of $13,485 for their services rendered during this account period. The conservator took $4,300 as fees on account after this account period and now hold the funds in a "set-aside" account. As of 01/05/2022, the estate cash balance is $2,785.37. No installment payment plan and other conditions of the payment, if the requested fees are to be allowed in full, have been proposed by the conservator. 2) The $800 assessment fee for the court investigator's review of this account remains unpaid. The conservator requests that the assessment fee be waived. 3) The conservator does not object to the Court's withdrawal of the conservator's authority to take fees on account as set out in the 09/08/2016 order. However, the conservator requests blanket authority to transfer funds to a set-aside account without petitioning ex parte for court authorization prior to each transfer. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HERBERT VILLAFLORES ARCEO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Whether the conservatee's trust should be under court supervision as conservatorship funds have been used to pay for the trust property and conservatorship funds have been transferred to the trust without a court order. It appears Ann O'Hara is trustee of the trust. 2) Court to determine how to proceed. Examiner requested receipts for entries on Schedule C Disbursements 3/22/2021 San Francisco Water, Power & Sewer/Water for $217.80 and $587.70. In response receipts were filed showing conservatorship funds being used to pay for trust property. 3) 03/30/2020 Ex Parte Order authorized $45,000.00 for repairs and maintenance to 426 Tocoloma Avenue, San Francisco CA. It appears $39,628.50 in repairs and maintenance were spent. Also, $4,992.16 on utilities which may be for 424 Tocoloma Avenue unclear. $588.42 were spent on property taxes for 424 Tocoloma Ave. $9,193.53 in mortgage payments were allegedly paid for 426 Tocoloma Ave and $481.32 in property taxes on 426 Tocoloma Ave.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELIPE R. VELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/14/2022 (no earlier date available): 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) Item 5 of the inventory and appraisal (property tax certificate) must be completed. 4) The inventory and appraisal did not mention the estate's 50% ownership of the real property. Please provide the probate referee's confirmation that the appraised value only reflects 50%. 5) Please provide a receipt or other proof of payment of the probate referee's commission and expenses. 6) Does any public entity have a claim against the estate under Probate Code section 9201? 7) The decedent died in 2008. Who has been paying expenses to maintain the estate's interest in the real property? 8) A legal description of the real property including the APN must be provided. 9) Are the disclaimers by the decedent's sister and her issue effective, even though they were signed and filed more than 9 months after the decedent's death? See section 279(b)(2). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHAPIRO FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) David Levin, the prior trustee, has not provided an accounting from the time that he served as trustee until Petitioner's appointment as successor trustee (6/8/19). Petitioner alleges that an accounting does not need to be filed by him, as he served prior to the trust being brought under court supervision. However, it appears that David Levin began to act as the successor trustee pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement, which was approved by the court on 11/1/18, and which stated that "David Levin shall be appointed as Successor Trustee and the Trust shall be brought under court supervision" Furthermore, it is unclear how this court can approve Mr. Levin's request for fees for the time he served when he has not provided an accounting for that time period. 2) Petitioner alleges that a check in the amount of $105,139.16 was written to Maxwell Shapiro, as conservator, in order to undo an unintentional IRA distribution (the $105,139.16 amount being the tax liability). Petitioner further alleges that the tax liability will be refunded back to the trust. 3) Petitioner requests an order that "the Court be informed and take notice" of payments made by Lise McCarthy, the alleged power of attorney for Sanford Shapiro, for various legal and professional expenses, including attorney fees, conservatee fees, and case management fees. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. At the 09/20/2021 hearing the court asked the parties if this an ancillary probate or a main probate. Mr. Saadeh stated this is the main probate. There is no original will filed or a certified copy. Instead, Marina Donskoy filed a Certificate Of Inheritance Under The Will, Issued To Verify The Right To Inheritance Outside The Russian Federation. 2) Tatiana's Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary requests Nikita Volchetvskiy be appointed administrator. Marina filed objections to the amended petition on 11/04/2021 alleging Nikita has a conflict of interest as CFO of the business and controlling the California assets. 3) Marina's Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed requests Leo F. Bautista, CLPF #61 be appointed administrator with will annexed. No objections have been filed to the second amended petition.

rulings.Case Title: TRUST FOR MICHAEL SWORDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #15, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/28/2022 (no earlier date available). As the supplement to the attorney's declaration increased the total amount of the attorney's fees and decreased the total amount of net cash distribution, the supplement is treated as an amendment requiring new 30-day notice. The amendment must be signed by both petitioners and verified by at least one of them. See CRC 7.104(a)(2). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
